blocks.content: Shirone headed to the integrated training ground early in the morning.
Starting today, classes were conducted in a tense atmosphere due to exam preparations.
The exam subjects varied by class.
Shirone's Class Five had a targeting test, commonly called the Speed Gun.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ugh! From today, it's just Speed Gun until we die. It's my worst subject. Targeting types have it easy. They're at such an advantage.

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: Not really. Targeting types are better against a few elite opponents. The practical exam targets pour out endlessly. Why not switch to offensive and blast everything in sight?

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: Then you'll just tire out first. Anyway, it's either offensive or targeting. No one uses evasion. How could you keep up with so many?

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: Speaking of which, what about Iruki? Is he using evasion again this time?

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: Haha! Seriously, only an idiot would do that. I'm curious about Shirone too. He's strong in actual combat. But since he's specialized in defense, I wonder what he'll choose.
The students' conversations occasionally mentioned Iruki and Shirone.
There were others with better grades, but the two were overwhelmingly expected to show something decisive when it mattered.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But Shirone wanted to cry. Far from being strong in actual combat, he hadn't even succeeded in photon emission yet. Without active magic, the practical exam would be a zero.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ugh! What should I do? I'm really worried.
The sound of a dog barking came from the edge of the training ground.
The girls noticed first, then the boys rushed over.

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: What's that? How did it get here?

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: Must be a wild dog from the mountains. But why is it barking like that?
As the crowd gathered, the dog grew more wary. It bared its teeth and growled.
Dogs were generally thought to wag their tails at people. But displaying violence made even a dog unpleasant to be around.

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: Hey! Get lost! You're disgusting!
Class Seven kids started throwing stones.
Upperclassmen watched but didn't intervene.
They thought it was no different from a wild animal since it disliked people.
But Shirone was different. After listening to the barking for a while, he realized something and ran over.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wait! Stop throwing stones.
Though he'd been their classmate not long ago, Shirone was now their senior, so the kids froze.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: H-hey! Stop it! Senior Shirone said to stop!
Shirone approached for a closer look. Mark followed nervously.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Senior, it's dangerous. This is a wild dog.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No matter how wild, dogs don't bite people. They'd rather run away.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: But it's barking like that.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Maybe…
Shirone fearlessly approached. The dog growled menacingly but didn't attack.
Shirone stared into its eyes and herded it toward the forest.
As expected, a puppy lay whimpering in the grass.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's hurt. Poor thing.

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: Huh? Really? Guys, there's a puppy here, a puppy!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't call them yet. Even a dog can turn dangerous when its pup is threatened.

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: Oh, right. Hey! Don't come closer!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Can you find someone who knows healing magic? It'd be great if Senior Seriel comes, but anyone from Class Five or below is fine.

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: No need. Maria can do it.
Maria, who'd attended the school since childhood, knew many types of magic.
Moreover, meeting Sard had given her peace of mind, so she got along well with her classmates.
When Mark called, she came running.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Oh my! How did this happen?

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: Probably got caught in a territorial fight. Can you fix it?

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Yes, Senior. This is easy.
Maria cast a healing spell, and the puppy's wounds closed.
Revitalized, it bounded around the grass.
The mother stopped barking and led the pup back into the mountains.
Saving a life was always heartwarming.
Even the stone-throwers watched the forest with emotion.

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: You're amazing, Senior. How did you know there was a puppy?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Haha! I used to live in the mountains.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone recalled a hunting trip with his father when he was about 11.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That day, they were lucky to find a red bear with valuable claws tangled in a rope.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A red bear's strength could snap the rope, so Vincent quickly nocked an arrow.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone waited nervously. Catching the bear would mean no worries about meals for a while.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But the arrow didn't fly. Puzzled, Shirone turned to see Vincent putting away his bow and preparing to leave.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Well, they say too much luck is bad. Let's head back, Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Why not take it? Mom would love it.
Vincent crouched and pointed where the bear was trapped.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Look there, Shirone.
Shirone looked closely. Beside the trapped mother, a cub whimpered.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: No matter how much of a hunter you are, you don't take a mother with cubs. Animals may be beasts, but their love for their young is no different from humans'.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But then we're going home empty-handed. That's a great catch right there.
Vincent stroked Shirone's head warmly.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: That bear has cubs just as I have you. As a parent, how could I ignore that? Fighting to survive is the same for humans and beasts. But you mustn't do it in front of your children. That's an unwritten rule for all living things.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That day, Shirone felt his father's denial. Perhaps Vincent hadn't spared the bear but protected his own child.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The certainty that his father would always protect him warmed Shirone's heart.
The students were moved by Shirone's story.
They didn't know it was about his father, but the impact was no less profound.

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: I see. Shirone, you're really cool.

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: Right. Shirone is so kind.
His classmates saw him differently.
They'd thought he was rigid and only cared about magic, but his warm heart was an unexpected charm.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: What foolish logic. No hunter spares prey for such reasons.
The warm mood was doused with cold water.
The kids glared at the speaker.
Iruki stood with his hands behind his back, looking at Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Not killing animals with cubs is to prevent the cubs from growing up to attack humans. In biology, it's called imprinting. Once they see humans as enemies, their offspring inherit the trait. That makes hunting difficult. But killing the cubs would deplete the species. It's the wisdom of hunters who can only hunt in their own territory.
Shirone was furious.
Of course, Iruki didn't know, but his words insulted his father's denial.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How can you say that? Sure, some hunters might think that way. But most spare animals out of genuine compassion.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Hearts. Emotions. These things are just packaging. I'm talking about reality. I don't dazzle people with foolish idealism like you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What? You've said enough!
Shirone jumped up and glared at Iruki.
It was the second round of their argument from the Supernatural Spiritual Science Research Club.
The students were stunned.
Shirone wasn't the type to fight, and Iruki didn't get along with anyone.
Their clash sent tension skyrocketing.

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: What's this? Did something happen between them? They act like they've fought before.

blocks.speaker: Unknown Student
blocks.content: They're rivals. They're both top candidates for promotion, after all.
Shirone glared with intensity.
Iruki, seemingly uninterested in fighting, looked around distractedly.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Hey, hey! What's with you two? You were quiet for a while. The teacher's coming, so let's go back.
Nade's intervention prevented a full-blown fight, but Shirone was left in a foul mood.
Iruki's mindset was incomprehensible.
Researching equations to wipe out cities, treating lives lightly—everything about him was disagreeable.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ugh, I shouldn't engage with him anymore.
Class began, and Etela came up the mountain.
The students gaped at her unusual appearance.
Perhaps because it was exam period?
She wore a training outfit with short sleeves and exposed thighs for ease of movement.
Most shocking was that she wasn't wearing glasses.
The boys were speechless at the beautiful woman before them.
Could glasses really change one's impression so much?

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: You know there's special training starting today, right? Due to the training ground's schedule, Class Five's Speed Gun test will be first. From tomorrow, each class will have their own time slots, so keep that in mind.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: Today's class will start with a demonstration by the assistant. Samuel, come here.
A tall, handsome blond man stepped forward.
He appeared to be in his early twenties, with unusually long hair for a man, reaching his waist.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: This assistant is Samuel, who's preparing for graduation in the senior class. His major is incantation magic, and he's here today to demonstrate magic.
Since it's related to Spirit Zone enhancement training, pay close attention.
Whoaaaaa!
The girls welcomed Samuel enthusiastically.
After feasting their eyes on the boys during integrated classes, the appearance of a handsome man was thrilling.

blocks.speaker: Samuel
blocks.content: Nice to meet you. It feels like just yesterday I was in the advanced class. Time flies. My skills are lacking, but I'll do my best to contribute to your education.
Shirone watched Samuel with interest.
This was a chance to see the rival competing with Amy in the senior class.
Especially since incantation magic was an unfamiliar major.
Samuel entered the Image Zone and expanded his Spirit Zone.
The students gasped.
A 42-meter-diameter sphere enveloped him, similar in size to Shirone's.

blocks.speaker: Samuel
blocks.content: Incantation magic strengthens magical power through the power of language. First, I'll cast Tornado without incantations. Tornado.
Samuel raised his hands, and a powerful whirlwind erupted.
Some in the advanced class could use Tornado, but Samuel cast it as easily as a basic spell.

blocks.speaker: Samuel
blocks.content: Remember the power of that. Now, I'll enhance Tornado with incantation magic. Literally using the power of language. By savoring the will embedded in words, a mage's mental strength grows.
Samuel cleared his throat and recited an eight-stanza poem.

blocks.speaker: Samuel
blocks.content: O land of fervor, lend me thy strength, caress my hand. The sky gazes down, and history is etched in moments. When the power of the void dwells in my palm…
Before the first stanza ended, the students' expressions soured.
The obscure, meaningless poem bored them.
Someone yawned, but Samuel wasn't offended and finished the poem.

blocks.speaker: Samuel
blocks.content: Tornado.
Whoooooosh!
A massive whirlwind shot skyward with a deafening roar.
The students froze, battered by wind so strong it stole their breath.
Samuel's figure wavered like a mirage.

blocks.speaker: Samuel
blocks.content: This is the power of incantation. Language carries a mage's will. Savoring that will strengthens magic. Speak, reflect. That's the basic framework of incantation magic. Now, let's begin in earnest.
